channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ✈️⛽ Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Summer Flights as Iran War Drives Jet Fuel Prices Higher
item.description: Germany's Lufthansa will eliminate 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer as jet fuel prices doubled since the US-Iran war began. The carrier is closing its CityLine service and canceling routes to 10 cities as the Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens Europe's fuel supply.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Countries to Revoke Flight Permits
item.description: President Lai Ching-te called off his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius, and Madagascar revoked his flight permits following what Taiwan calls "intense pressure" from Beijing. It's the first time a Taiwanese leader has had to cancel a trip due to revoked flight permits.

item.title: 🚢🇮🇷 Iran Seizes Two Cargo Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Blockade Standoff Continues
item.description: Iranian forces attacked and seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, with one cargo ship reported as "heavily damaged." The escalation comes as Iran says reopening the strait is "impossible" if the US blockade continues, keeping the Gulf shipping chokepoint on edge.

item.title: 🇲🇩⚖️ Moldova's Former Most Powerful Person Sentenced to 19 Years in Jail
item.description: Moldovan oligarch Ilan Shor was sentenced in absentia for corruption and fraud, marking a major victory for Moldova's anti-corruption efforts. Shor fled to Israel and remains a fugitive.

item.title: 🇩🇪⚔️ Germany Unveils Strategy to Become Europe's Strongest Military by 2039
item.description: Germany announced an ambitious military modernization plan to rebuild its armed forces into Europe's most capable fighting force within 15 years, reflecting the strategic shift prompted by Russia's threat and the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇳🇱⚔️ Russia Could Be Ready for NATO Conflict Year After Ukraine War Ends, Dutch Warn
item.description: Dutch intelligence warns that Russia could rebuild its military to threaten NATO within a year after the Ukraine war ends, urging allies to maintain defense spending and readiness.

item.title: 🇨🇦🇺🇸 Canada PM Hits Back After Trump Team Demands Entry Fee for Trade Talks
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney rejected US demands for concessions before trade negotiations, warning that Canada "won't be dictated to" amid escalating US-Canada trade tensions.

item.title: 🇪🇪🇱🇻🇱🇹 Baltic Nations Brace for Impact of Iran War, Delaying US Weapons Shipments
item.description: Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania face delayed US military deliveries as resources shift to the Middle East, raising concerns about NATO's eastern flank amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Fires on 3 Ships in Strait of Hormuz as US Maintains Blockade
item.description: Iran attacked three vessels in the strategic Strait of Hormuz as the US naval blockade continues. The ceasefire extension hasn't stopped maritime tensions in the critical shipping lane.

item.title: 🇳🇬⚖️ Nigeria's Suspected Coup Plotters Deny Treason Charges
item.description: Nigerian military officers accused of plotting a coup have pleaded not guilty to treason charges. The case follows last year's cancellation of Independence Day celebrations amid coup fears.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Approves €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine as Pipeline Resumes
item.description: The EU approved a massive loan package for Ukraine while the Druzhba oil pipeline reopened, ending a months-long deadlock over stalled energy supplies to Hungary.

item.title: ⛪🌍 Pope Criticizes Equatorial Guinea Prisons as Africa Tour Winds Down
item.description: Pope Leo delivered a frank speech on prison conditions in Equatorial Guinea, continuing his critical stance during his Africa tour.

item.title: 🇺🇸🔍 Justice Department Withdraws Subpoenas in John Brennan Investigation
item.description: In a reversal, the Justice Department has withdrawn subpoenas related to the investigation of former CIA Director John Brennan, according to AP sources.

item.title: 🌊⚔️ Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
item.description: NATO launched its first coordinated response to suspected infrastructure sabotage after a deep-sea cable was damaged in European waters, reflecting growing concerns about hybrid warfare.

item.title: 🇨🇩⚔️ Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo Safe Haven City
item.description: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels entered Goma, a humanitarian hub in eastern Congo that was supposed to be a safe zone, escalating the regional conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇹🇸🇦 Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining GCAP Fighter Jet Program
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Meloni confirmed Italy's support for Saudi Arabia joining the Global Combat Air Programme, expanding the sixth-generation fighter jet partnership.

item.title: 🌍🏛️ Climate Change Threat to Democracy and Elections, Says Guardian Report
item.description: Extreme weather events are increasingly disrupting elections worldwide and threatening democratic stability, according to a comprehensive analysis of climate impacts.

item.title: ⛪🌍 Pope Leo's Africa Visit Snubbed by Regional Leaders
item.description: Pope Leo's Africa tour faced diplomatic cold shoulders from some regional leaders, highlighting complex religious-political dynamics on the continent.

item.title: 🇮🇷🚢 Iran Ramps Up Ship Attacks in Hormuz After Trump Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Iran has increased attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz despite the ceasefire extension, targeting shipping in the critical waterway.

item.title: 🇺🇸✈️ USAF Doubles F-15EX Buy, Making Strategic Sense
item.description: The US Air Force has doubled its F-15EX fighter procurement, a move analysts say makes strategic sense given capacity needs and production constraints.

item.title: 🇮🇷💣 Iran's Mines in Hormuz Could Take Six Months to Clear
item.description: Naval mines deployed by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz could require up to six months of clearance operations, complicating any reopening of the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇨🇳 Israel's Dependence on Chinese Drones Exposes IDF Vulnerability
item.description: Israel's military reliance on Chinese-made drones has created strategic vulnerabilities as the Iran conflict stretches Israeli defense resources.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Iran War Has Depleted Half of US Patriot and THAAD Stockpiles
item.description: The US Iran conflict has consumed approximately 50% of America's Patriot and THAAD missile defense interceptors, raising concerns about ammunition sustainability.
